cards.front: What is the 2.2 Ch in time series analysis?
cards.back: The 2.2 Ch stands for the Chi-Square Statistic, used to determine the significance of autocorrelation in time series

cards.front: What is the purpose of the 2.2 Ch calculation?
cards.back: The purpose of the 2.2 Ch calculation is to test the null hypothesis that the autocovariance function is equal to zero

cards.front: What is the 2.2 Ch formula used to calculate?
cards.back: The 2.2 Ch formula calculates the Chi-Square statistic, which is used to determine the significance of autocorrelation

cards.front: What is the significance level in 2.2 Ch?
cards.back: The significance level in 2.2 Ch is typically set at 0.05, meaning that if the calculated Chi-Square statistic exceeds this value, the null hypothesis is rejected

cards.front: What is the result of a 2.2 Ch test?
cards.back: The result of a 2.2 Ch test is either a rejection of the null hypothesis (indicating significant autocorrelation) or failure to reject the null hypothesis (indicating no significant autocorrelation)
